message:
feat(web_framework): Cx-first start_with_cx + finish_with_cx + deepen cx threading (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Deepens tick 100's web-lifecycle cx-awareness from pre-flight-only
to framework-level threading:

1. Add `FrameworkWebRuntime::start_with_cx(cx, bind_addr, app)` in
   web_framework.rs. Two checkpoint seams (before startup hooks,
   before bind) plus — critically — threads the caller's cx into
   the spawned accept loop instead of spawning a fresh
   per-request cx. Every accepted connection now inherits the
   caller's capability context.

2. Add `FrameworkWebRuntime::finish_with_cx(cx, result)` in
   web_framework.rs. Drains connections + runs shutdown hooks
   unconditionally (safety invariant), then checkpoints to let a
   cancelled caller return early to its parent.

3. Upgrade `start_web_server_with_cx` (tick 100) from simple
   delegate to route through `FrameworkWebRuntime::start_with_cx`,
   inlining the bind validation + startup log.

4. Upgrade `WebServerHandle::shutdown_with_cx` (tick 100) to route
   through `finish_with_cx` so the drain+hooks phase is also
   cx-aware.

Legacy `start`, `finish`, `start_web_server`, `shutdown` all
preserved unchanged.

Regression test `web_health_with_cx_ephemeral_port` (tick 100)
continues to pass — verifies the full cx-threaded startup +
shutdown path roundtrips a /health GET identically to the legacy
pair.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features web,asupersync-runtime):
2/2 web integration tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
